text:
OlympicchampionJessica Foxhas apologised to her dad after putting him down with a ‘really mean’ dig after winning gold inParis.

The 30-year-old slalom canoeist ‘completed’ the Olympics by winning gold in the K1 event in Paris, having won silver at London 2012 and bronze at the previous two Games.

There was more reason for the Fox family to celebrate in Paris as Jessica’s sister Noemie also won gold in the first ever women’s slalom kayak cross event.

It’s hardly surprising that Jessica and Noemie have developed into world-class, Olympic-winning athletes as both their parents were also stars of the canoeing world.

Mum Myriam Fox-Jerusalmi competed at the international level from 1979 to 1996 and won bronze in the K1 at the Atlanta Olympics in 1996.

The French star also won ten medals at the ICF Canoe Slalom World Championships and the World Cup title three consecutive times between 1989 and 1991.

Dad Richard, meanwhile, competed for Great Britain for the best part of two decades, winning ten gold medals at the World Championships.

But Richard is the only family member without an Olympic medal, having finished fourth and narrowly missing out on silverware in the 1992 Olympics in Barcelona.

Speaking to Australian TV following sister Noemie’s Olympic gold, Jessica poked fun at her dad’s misfortunate, saying: ‘It means that poor dad is the only one without an Olympic medal!

‘What a moment for our family. What a moment for these games.’

Almost 24 hours after the dig, Jessica issued a public apology to her dad, insisting he should proud of his ‘great resume’ despite his Olympic disappointment.

‘I was pretty savage in my interview, and I realised after that was really mean,’ she told Australian broadcaster Channel Nine.

‘Sorry, Dad. You have a great resume, 10-time world champion, amazing! We’ve got four gold medals in the family, so there’s one for everyone.’

title: Olympic champion apologises to her dad for 'savage' comment after winning gold | Metro News
description: Olympic champion Jessica Fox has apologised to her dad after putting him down with a 'really mean' dig after winning gold at Paris 2024.
